summary: Applied Data Scientist specializing in high-throughput biological data and production analytics systems with extensive experience designing end-to-end data workflows, developing custom R packages, and building cloud-backed applications. Skilled in Python, SQL, AWS, and statistical modeling, with a strong foundation in experimental design and high-throughput data. Proven track record of optimizing data analysis pipelines, improving lab efficiency, and delivering actionable insights to drive research and operational success.

experience.title: Lead Data Scientist
highlights: Architected and deployed end-to-end data analysis pipelines to process high-throughput diagnostic datasets (up to 100 samples/week, ~240k rows of time-series data/project), reducing analysis time from ~4 hours to seconds.

Led full-stack development of a 	extbf{Python}-based LIMS (PriLIMS) using 	extbf{Django}, automating client data intake, tracking, and reporting, eliminating 10 hours/week of manual administrative work.
Implemented cloud-based infrastructure on AWS to support scalable SQL storage and analysis, improving system reliability and enabling secure multi-user access.
Introduced Git-based version control and CI workflows across projects, improving collaboration efficiency and significantly reducing deployment errors.

experience.title: Researcher IV
experience.organization: University of Minnesota, Center for Prion Research and Outreach
highlights: Developed a custom R package and automation scripts to standardize RT-QuIC data analysis, reducing hours of analysis time to seconds and increasing reproducibility across collaborating labs.
Streamlined data processing and reporting workflows using R and Python tools, reducing turnaround time for experimental results by 99%.
Built and evaluated statistical models to classify diagnostic outcomes, improving disease detection confidence.
Standardized cross-lab analytics and built reusable analytical frameworks adopted by multiple institutions.
Led laboratory establishment, expansion, and workflow redesign, increasing research capacity.
